---
record_type: bill
bill_id: bill.repair_ledger_faucet_parts
alias: repair_ledger_faucet_parts
purchased_on: 2026-01-03
total_eur: 32
counterparty: Hörnbach Österreich
project: Repair Ledger
related_entity: Petra Novak
lines:
  - item: faucet cartridge
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 24
    line_eur: 24
  - item: seal set
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 8
    line_eur: 8
---

| related_entity | Petra Novak                     |
+----------------+---------------------------------+
```

## Line Items

+---+------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item             | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | faucet cartridge | 1   | 24       | 24       |
| 2 | seal set         | 1   | 8        | 8        |

```

## Notes

Exactly the kind of household fix that becomes folklore unless Miles captures the receipt and part numbers.

function.message: Processed the oldest inbox message by OCRing all Petra Novak-related bill records into YAML frontmatter and deleting the completed inbox item.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed bootstrap requirements, read nested AGENTS when entering folders, used inbox and OCR/migration workflows, verified Petra Novak against the canonical entity record, preserved note bodies while adding schema-compliant finance frontmatter, and deleted the inbox item only after completing the request.
function.outcome_justification: The inbox request in 00_inbox/000_next-task.md asked to OCR all bills related to Petra Novak. Canonical identity was verified in 10_entities/cast/petra.md. Petra-related bill records were evidenced by the two purchase files read, each of which visibly contained related_entity Petra Novak and complete bill fields/line items needed by the schema in 99_system/schemas/finance-record-frontmatter.md. Those two records were updated in place with YAML frontmatter per the OCR and migration workflows, and the processed inbox item was then deleted per 00_inbox/AGENTS.MD and 99_system/workflows/process-next-inbox-item.md.
